articles.description:
General atmosphere during Grindr White House Correspondents' Dinner Weekend Party 2026 at LXIV DC on April 24, 2026 in Washington, DC. | Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images for Grindr Inc.	

Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and technology learning how to politick. If you're not a subscriber but would like to support our work, please subscribe here. I promise that your money will not go toward paying for a drone-proof ballroom for The Verge staff, no matter how much fun we'd have throwing parties there.
Speaking of parties: The Verge normally wouldn't do a party report from the White House Correspondents' Dinner week, also known as "Nerd Prom," because it's a bit too much Washington insider circle-jerking for normal people to stomach. (This year was weirder than most, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Splatoon Raiders preorders for the Switch 2 are nearly 20 percent off
articles.description:
Nintendo recently announced a new pricing policy, which knocks $10 off the cost of digital versions of future first-party titles exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2. Splatoon Raiders, for instance, is available for preorder ahead of its July 23rd release for $49.99 digitally or $59.99 for the physical edition. However, Walmart is the exception in how it’s pricing preorders for the cartridge version. Right now, you can reserve the upcoming for $49.94 — just a few cents cheaper than the digital version. Raiders isn’t a sequel to Splatoon 3, but rather a spinoff that has similar third-person, paint-splatting gameplay set in new locales.
Splatoon Raiders

Where to Buy:

 $59.99 $49.94 at Walmart (physical)
 $59.99 at Amazon (physical)

The same $10 discount is in effect for Yoshi and the Mysterious Book, a side-scrolling adventure with a gorgeous storybook art style that comes out on May 21st. The price of the physical version is $69.99, but Walmart is currently offering it for $59.88, which is similar to the digital edition’s price. Given that prices on many tech and gaming-related products are increasing, it’s nice to see notable discounts on physical Switch 2 games. If you’re looking for other gaming deals, we’ve pulled together the best discounts from Amazon’s weeklong Gaming Week sale.
Yoshi and the Mysterious Book

Where to Buy:

 $69.99 $59.88 at Walmart (physical)
 $69.99 at Amazon (physical)

articles.title: Microsoft reports sinking Xbox revenue as its cloud business climbs
articles.description:
Microsoft's Xbox hardware revenue continues to tumble, with the company revealing a 33 percent decline as part of its earnings report released on Wednesday. Even though the rest of Microsoft's consumer-focused division took a dip, the company's cloud and productivity businesses continue to soar, driving the company toward $82.9 billion in revenue.
Along with declining Xbox hardware revenue, Microsoft reported a 5 percent drop in Xbox content and services as well. Microsoft has seen a significant amount of executive turnover over the past few months, with the retirement of Xbox chief CEO Phil Spencer and the departure of former Xbox presiden …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Kimmel reacts sharply to Trump’s ‘we won’t match that record’ marriage joke during King Charles event

Turning to first lady Melania Trump, he added: “That’s a record we won’t be able to match, darling, I’m sorry. Just not going to work out that way.”

articles.title: Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
articles.description:
Google Search queries hit an "all time high" in the first quarter of 2026, according to a statement from CEO Sundar Pichai published as part of Alphabet's earnings on Wednesday.
"Our AI investments and full stack approach are lighting up every part of the business," Pichai says. "Search had a strong quarter with AI experiences driving usage, queries at an all time high, and 19% revenue growth." He also notes that Q1 was "our strongest quarter ever for our consumer AI plans, driven by the Gemini App" and that the company now has more than 350 million paid subscriptions, with "YouTube and Google One being the key drivers."
Alphabet also anno …
Read the full story at The Verge.

This performance highlights Alphabet's significant gains in the competitive AI market, bolstered by its Gemini models and a key partnership with Apple.

Microsoft is expanding its AI offerings, including partnerships, to maintain its competitive edge in the cloud market. This comes as major tech firms invest heavily in AI infrastructure.

articles.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
articles.description:
Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status 
The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin.
During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
articles.description:
The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents are circulating from the earliest days of OpenAI - and from before the AI lab even had a name. Some high-level takeaways: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang gave OpenAI an in-demand supercomputer, Musk largely drafted OpenAI's mission and heavily influenced its early structure, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman appeared to want to lean heavily on Y Combinator for early support for OpenAI, OpenAI president Greg Brockman and Ilya Sutskever worried about Musk's level of con …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.description: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta is working on AI agents for personal and business use
articles.description: The CEO wants to make an agent so easy even his mom can use it.

articles.title: Fecal vandal's nearly weeklong crime spree comes to an end when police catch her in the act
articles.description: Alexis Weber, 50, was arrested in Altamonte Springs, Florida, after police allegedly caught her defecating in her yard amid a neighborhood poop spree.

articles.title: WATCH:  Viral menopause hack raises medical questions
articles.description: Dr. Jennifer Miao explains the risks and science behind a viral trend using Allegra and Pepcid to ease menopause symptoms.

articles.title: Newsom mocks Trump's limited-edition passport with fake California driver's license featuring gov's portrait
articles.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom mocks President Trump's limited-edition U.S. passport by jokingly unveiling a driver's license featuring his own potrait.

articles.title: Japanese airline starts testing robot baggage handlers, and the early returns are not impressive
articles.description: Japan Airlines is rolling out humanoid robots at Tokyo's Haneda Airport for baggage handling, but the early results are far from impressive so far.

articles.title: Trump Says US Reviewing Possible Reduction Of Its Troops In Germany
articles.description: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that the United States is considering reducing the number of its troops in Germany, amid a row with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the Iran war.

articles.title: U.S. carrier in Iran war set to leave in days as official reveals conflict's cost
articles.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier is expected to leave the Middle East in the coming days, a U.S. official confirmed to CBS News.

articles.title: Elon Musk tells court he was a "fool" for funding OpenAI
articles.description: Elon Musk alleges that OpenAI reneged on a promise to operate as a nonprofit dedicated to human progress.

articles.title: "I Literally Was A Fool": Elon Musk Grilled In OpenAI Trial
articles.description: Elon Musk faced fiery questioning Wednesday in his court showdown with OpenAI, as he insisted that the maker of ChatGPT had fooled him by turning what was an altruistic pursuit into a profit-making...

articles.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
articles.description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. Ahead of OpenAI’s highly anticipated IPO, the court could rule on whether the company is allowed to exist as a for-profit enterprise and might even oust…

dailyNews.title: 'Get off the plane,' Delta passengers chant at flyer who refuses to hang up phone
dailyNews.description: A Delta flight passenger refused to end a cellphone call while taxiing in Miami, leading to disruptive behavior and removal from the Atlanta-bound flight.

dailyNews.title: Democrats hold 10-point midterm advantage, poll finds
dailyNews.description: New polling from Emerson College shows Democrats with an advantage ahead of the midterms. Former Democratic Rep. Max Rose of New York and Republican strategist Joe Borelli join with analysis.

dailyNews.title: James Comey makes first court appearance for charges in connection with social media post
dailyNews.description: Former FBI Director James Comey made his first court appearance in Virginia on Wednesday, charged with threatening President Trump. A grand jury indicted Comey yesterday over a social media post he made last year. CBS News Department of Justice reporter Jake Rosen has more.

dailyNews.title: Amazon earnings beat expectations with strong cloud growth
dailyNews.description: Revenue in Amazon's cloud computing segment expanded 28% year over year, topping analysts' estimates.

dailyNews.title: STAT+: Supreme Court justices appear to signal ‘skinny labeling’ can survive
dailyNews.description: At Wednesday's argument, the justices did not appear to question whether skinny labeling is valid.

dailyNews.title: Tiffany Henyard was reportedly living in Georgia while still serving as Dolton mayor, collecting pay
dailyNews.description: Former Dolton "super mayor" Tiffany Henyard reportedly began living in Georgia while still serving as the mayor in Illinois after losing her election in 2025.

dailyNews.title: Fed Meeting Underscores Tough Task Ahead for Warsh
dailyNews.description: Jerome H. Powell on Wednesday announced he would stay on as a governor at the central bank as internal divisions sharpen about the policy path forward.

dailyNews.description: Also, the Pentagon estimates the Iran war cost at $25 billion. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

dailyNews.title: US Stock Futures Fall on Tech Earnings, Oil Gains: Markets Wrap
dailyNews.description: US equity-index futures fell early Thursday after a mixed picture from tech giants reporting late Wednesday in the US failed to ease concerns over AI spending.

dailyNews.title: Grindr — yes, Grindr — won the WHCD party circuit
dailyNews.description:
General atmosphere during Grindr White House Correspondents' Dinner Weekend Party 2026 at LXIV DC on April 24, 2026 in Washington, DC. | Tasos Katopodis/Getty Images for Grindr Inc.	

Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and technology learning how to politick. If you're not a subscriber but would like to support our work, please subscribe here. I promise that your money will not go toward paying for a drone-proof ballroom for The Verge staff, no matter how much fun we'd have throwing parties there.

dailyNews.title: US Seeks to Deploy Hypersonic Missile For the First Time Against Iran
dailyNews.description: US Central Command has asked to send the Army’s long-delayed Dark Eagle hypersonic missile to the Middle East for possible use against Iran, seeking a longer-range system to hit ballistic-missile launchers deep inside the country.

topStories.description: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said he believes NASA has a good chance of returning astronauts to the Moon's surface before he leaves the White House at the start of 2029.

topStories.title: Trump says Iran ‘choking like a stuffed pig,’ vows naval blockade will remain until nuclear deal
topStories.description: Trump framed the naval blockade at the Strait of Hormuz as a central tool of leverage against Tehran, even suggesting it could be more effective than military strikes.

topStories.title: Alleged Trump Assassin Took Selfie Moments Before Attack: Prosecutors
topStories.description: The man accused of trying to assassinate President Donald Trump took a selfie in his hotel room moments before bursting through security with a pump-action shotgun, prosecutors said Wednesday.

topStories.title: Cole Allen case reveals Secret Service failures that could have led to tragedy at D.C. gala
topStories.description: New details about alleged gunman Cole Allen's dash into the White House correspondents' dinner while President Trump and other Cabinet officials were in attendance has raised hard questions for the U.S. Secret Service.

topStories.title: Secret Service agent was hit by gunfire from suspect at W.H. correspondents’ dinner, sources say
topStories.description: Investigators believe that the man charged with the attempted assassination of President Donald Trump shot a Secret Service officer who was wearing a ballistics vest, according to three law enforcement officials familiar with the case

topStories.title: Kimmel reacts sharply to Trump’s ‘we won’t match that record’ marriage joke during King Charles event
topStories.description: During a formal reception welcoming King Charles, Trump reflected on his parents’ long marriage, noting they had been together for 63 years. Turning to first lady Melania Trump, he added: “That’s a record we won’t be able to match, darling, I’m sorry. Just not going to work out that way.”

dailyNews.title: Hegseth clashes with Democrats over Iran war, dismissal of top military leaders
dailyNews.description: The defense secretary sought to rebut lawmakers’ criticism of the Trump administration’s handling of the conflict, which has cost about $25 billion so far.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court Grapples With Trump’s Plan to Revoke Deportation Protections
dailyNews.description: The case deals with Temporary Protected Status for hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians but could have implications for more than a million from troubled nations.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he told Putin to end Ukraine war, not focus on Iran's uranium enrichment
dailyNews.description: Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump described the call as productive and said the two leaders discussed multiple global issues, with Ukraine remaining the central focus.

dailyNews.title: ABC can beat Trump FCC's license threat if owner Disney is willing to fight
dailyNews.description: Broadcast license renewals are "all but automatic" due to 1996 change in US law.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  Trump hosts Artemis II crew at White House after historic lunar flyby mission
dailyNews.description: President Trump welcomed the Artemis II crew to the White House after they completed a record-breaking trip around the moon.

dailyNews.title: Jerome Powell calls his time as Fed chair a 'privilege' during last press conference
dailyNews.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said his time as head of the central bank has been a "privilege," as he gave his last press conference as chair.  The Senate Banking Committee advanced President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Powell, Kevin Warsh. Powell will remain at the Fed after May 15, when his tenure as its chair ends.

dailyNews.title: Brent oil tops $118 after Trump says he will blockade Iran until it agrees to a nuclear deal
dailyNews.description: The latest move higher comes amid reports that the U.S. will look to extend its blockade of Iranian ports.

dailyNews.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
dailyNews.description:
Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status 
The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin.
During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case.
 Continue reading...

dailyNews.title: Trump EPA chief vows he won’t take ‘morality lessons’ from Dem senator after heated clash
dailyNews.description: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse traded barbs over coal plant consumer costs on Wednesday amid Trump's proposed 52% cut to EPA funding for 2027.

dailyNews.title: Melania Trump advisor reveals what first lady was thinking as Secret Service rushed WHCA Dinner
dailyNews.description: Melania Trump was "in full control" during the assassination attempt at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, her senior advisor tells reporters.

dailyNews.title: CNN's Jake Tapper defends Jimmy Kimmel after telling ESPN to 'shut down' Aaron Rodgers for joking about Kimmel
dailyNews.description: CNN's Jake Tapper applied a wildly different standard when defending Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump while previously condemning Aaron Rodgers for joking about Kimmel.

dailyNews.title: Melania Trump, Queen Camilla and the Look of the Special Relationship
dailyNews.description: Parsing a state visit told in photo ops, and style.
